summary: Scottish author known for The Prime of Miss Jean Brodie, Memento Mori, The Girls of Slender Means, The Comforters, and The Ballad of Peckham Rye. Her conversion to Catholicism and breakdown involving T.S. Eliot influenced her early fiction.

works.name: The Prime of Miss Jean Brodie
works.description: Spark's best-known novel, set at the Marcia Blaine School for Girls. The title schoolteacher selects six bright girls as her chosen 'set,' calling them the 'creme de la creme.' Sandy Stranger has an affair with the art teacher Teddy Lloyd. Sandy becomes a nun (Sister Helena) after helping Miss Mackay get Brodie fired for having fascist sympathies. Sandy writes Transfiguration of the Commonplace. Joyce Emily Hammond is inspired by Brodie to fight in the Spanish Civil War and dies. Miss Brodie is accused of 'thinking she is the God of Calvin.'

clues.clue: Sandy Stranger becomes a nun after helping get Miss Brodie fired for fascist sympathies
examples: Sandy Stranger becomes a nun after helping Miss Mackay get her rival fired

works.name: Memento Mori
works.description: Characters receive mysterious phone calls from an unidentified individual who simply says 'Remember you must die.' Features Charmian, who constantly forgets that her former maid Jean Taylor now lives in a nursing home. The anthropologist Alec Warner's work is destroyed in a fire. Dame Lettie Colson starts receiving the calls. Set partly in the Maud Long Medical Ward.

clues.clue: Characters receive phone calls saying 'Remember you must die'
examples: characters receive phone calls from a mysterious individual who simply says, 'Remember you must die'

clues.clue: Charmian forgets her maid Jean Taylor lives in a nursing home; Alec Warner's work destroyed in a fire
examples: Charmian, who constantly forgets that her former maid Jean Taylor now lives in a nursing home

works.name: The Girls of Slender Means
works.description: Set at the May of Teck Club. The women share a Schiaparelli dress as a good-luck token. Nicholas Farringdon becomes a missionary and is executed in Haiti after being inspired by another character's piety. Selina rushes back into a crumbling house to retrieve a dress instead of helping the elocution instructor Joanna Childe, who dies due to an unexploded bomb. The title refers to both the economic and physical stature of the characters. Jane Wright investigates Farringdon's death.

works.name: The Comforters and Other Works
works.description: Spark suffered a breakdown early in her career in which she believed T.S. Eliot's play The Confidential Clerk contained secret messages written to her in Greek. This, along with her conversion to Catholicism and abuse of Dexedrine, inspired The Comforters, in which Caroline Rose is haunted by a 'Typing Ghost' who tells her she is a fictional character. The Ballad of Peckham Rye features Dougal Douglas, who is associated with the devil due to lumps from surgically removed horns, and opens with Humphrey Place leaving his fiancee at the altar. The Mandelbaum Gate follows Barbara Vaughan traveling through an Israeli checkpoint to marry an archaeologist in Jerusalem. Anthony Burgess included The Mandelbaum Gate among his favorite English novels. 'The Seraph and the Zambesi' is one of her short stories. Aiding and Abetting features two characters who believe they are Lord Lucan. She was Scottish.

clues.clue: Suffered a breakdown believing T.S. Eliot's Confidential Clerk contained secret messages; inspired The Comforters
examples: suffered a breakdown in which she believed T.S. Eliot's play The Confidential Clerk contained secret messages

comprehensive_summary:
Muriel Spark was a Scottish author whose conversion to Catholicism and early mental breakdown profoundly influenced her fiction. She believed T.S. Eliot's play The Confidential Clerk contained secret messages to her in Greek, an experience that, combined with her Dexedrine abuse, inspired her first novel The Comforters, in which Caroline Rose is haunted by a 'Typing Ghost.'

Her most famous work is The Prime of Miss Jean Brodie, set at the Marcia Blaine School for Girls, where the charismatic title teacher selects six girls as her 'set' and calls them the 'creme de la creme.' Sandy Stranger has an affair with art teacher Teddy Lloyd, later becomes the nun Sister Helena (writing Transfiguration of the Commonplace), and helps Miss Mackay get Brodie fired for fascist sympathies. Joyce Emily Hammond is inspired by Brodie to fight and die in the Spanish Civil War.

Memento Mori features elderly characters receiving mysterious phone calls saying 'Remember you must die,' with Charmian forgetting her maid Jean Taylor lives in a nursing home. The Girls of Slender Means is set at the May of Teck Club, where women share a Schiaparelli dress. Joanna Childe dies from an unexploded bomb, Nicholas Farringdon is later executed as a missionary in Haiti, and Selina prioritizes retrieving a dress over helping Joanna.

The Ballad of Peckham Rye features Dougal Douglas, associated with the devil due to lumps from surgically removed horns. The Mandelbaum Gate follows Barbara Vaughan traveling through an Israeli checkpoint. Aiding and Abetting features two characters who believe they are Lord Lucan.
